FCTI & Sim-P
Agreement 2026
The complete collective bargaining agreement for Flight Crew Training Instructors and Simulator Pilot Instructors - now actually readable. You're welcome.

Pick a category, click any article, read it like a normal human being.
⚠️ About the Plain English explainers
The 📌 Plain English buttons are informal summaries written to help make sense of the legalese — not legal advice, not written by a lawyer, and not binding on anyone. They may contain errors or simplifications. The actual contract language is what governs. When in doubt, read the real contract or talk to your union rep.
✈️
Your Job
Recognition, definitions, classification, probation
✈️1. Recognition and ScopeWho is covered, and why everyone else is jealous.📖2. DefinitionsForty-seven articles and we start with a dictionary. Classic.🧪9. Probationary Period90 days of "we can fire you for literally anything." Try not to be weird.🏅11. Classifications and QualificationsFCTI vs. Sim-P: same job, different sim, deeply held opinions about both.
🕐
Your Schedule
Hours, bidding, overtime, holidays, sick calls
🕐3. Hours of ServiceHow many days you work, when, and all the ways that can change.⏰6. OvertimeWork more, get paid more. Groundbreaking concept, beautifully lawyered.🎉7. HolidaysTen whole days the company can't make you feel guilty. In theory.📞24. Absence from DutyYou're sick. Here's exactly who to call and when. No excuses.✈️26. Field Work & Out of Base AssignmentsHotel per diem, out-of-base sims, and travel days. The away game rules.📋LOA: Reserve Day (W Day) LanguageWhat W Day means — assignment rules and notice requirements📋LOA: Art. 3(J)(5) Hours of ServiceBack-fill and mandatory coverage rules, officially defined
💵
Your Pay
Compensation, profit sharing, severance, retirement
💵4. CompensationThe part you skip to first. We see you.📈5. Profit SharingIf the airline prints money, you get a sliver. Better than nothing!💼37. Severance AllowanceA parting gift on the way out. It won't cover rent, but it's the thought.🌅40. Retirement Benefits5% in, 4% match. Future you says: thank you for not touching this.📋LOA: Art. 7(E) Holiday Pay LanguageWhen holiday premium pay applies and how it's calculated
🩺
Your Benefits
Medical, dental, vision, life insurance, fitness
🩺41. InsuranceGet sick, get fixed, don't go bankrupt. The American Dream, apparently.💪39. Fitness for DutyYou operate million-dollar machines. Yes, they care if you can see straight.🏦40. Retirement Benefits401(k), employer match, vesting. The money you stop working to spend someday.📋LOA: Sick Leave → HRA ConversionConvert accrued sick leave to healthcare dollars at retirement
🏖️
Time Off & Leave
Vacation, sick leave, bereavement, jury duty
🏖️8. VacationEarn it, bid it, actually take it this time. You deserve it.🏥34. Sick LeaveGet sick, use it. Don't get sick, bank it. Cash it out when you leave. Win-win-win.🕊️21. BereavementThree paid days when the unthinkable happens. Small, but it's something.⚖️22. Jury DutyCivic duty on company time. Your country thanks you. Your manager, less so.
🔄
Career Moves
Seniority, transfers, reclassification, recall
📋10. SeniorityThe hierarchy is real. The pecking order has receipts.🔄12. Reclassification and TransferSick of your fleet? Want a new base? Good luck -- it's complicated.📃13. System Seniority ListThe official list. Your number is either satisfying or soul-crushing.⚠️14. Loss of SeniorityThe ways your hard-earned seniority can evaporate. Don't do these things.📉15. Reduction in ForceLayoffs: the most carefully negotiated way to tell you "it's not you, it's budget."🔔16. RecallCalled back from furlough. Dust off your badge, they need you again.
🚪
Leaves & Exit
Leaves of absence, military, resignations
🌴17. Leaves of AbsenceLegitimate reasons to disappear for a while. We won't tell.🎖️18. Military LeaveServe your country. Your job will be here when you get back. Probably.🚪19. Termination of EmploymentHow employment ends. Spoiler: give two weeks' notice or you lose vacation.📦44. Moving ExpensesPack up, move out, submit receipts. AA covers the U-Haul.
🤝
Your Rights
Non-discrimination, representation, union, no-strike
📌20. Bulletin BoardsThe union gets a corkboard. Revolutionary.🔧27. General ProvisionsLockers, parking, bathrooms, phones. The glamorous stuff nobody brags about.🤝28. Non-Discrimination and Recognition of RightsNo discrimination. Not a suggestion. Not a vibe. A legally binding fact.🗣️29. RepresentationYour right to have a human in your corner during the scary stuff.🚫33. No Strike / No LockoutNo walkouts. No lockouts. Everybody shows up. End of conversation.🔗38. Union SecurityDues, membership, and why your paycheck is slightly smaller. Totally worth it.
⚖️
Dispute Process
Discipline, grievances, System Board, duration
🎓23. Company Hearings and TrainingIf they make you sit through it, they have to pay for it. Simple math.🔥30. Grievance Procedure for DisciplineGot fired or written up? Here's where you fight back. Politely. Procedurally.📝31. Grievance Procedure for Contract DisputesThink the company broke the contract? Here's the official complaint form. Metaphorically.🏛️32. Board of AdjustmentThe nuclear option. Four lawyers walk into a room. Nobody wins. Except lawyers.🔖45. Effect on Prior AgreementsWhatever the last contract said? This one beats it. Full stop.📅47. DurationEffective 2026, expires 2031. See you at the bargaining table.
